claimText: Maharashtra has signed an agreement with an IIT Madras startup to build a hyperloop connecting JNPT to Vadhavan Port.
evidenceExcerpt: Maharashtra inked a deal with an IIT Madras startup to build a hyperloop connecting JNPT to Vadhavan Port.
examUse: Current Affairs statement-based questions regarding recent infrastructure and mobility projects in India.
distractorHooks: IIT Bombay instead of IIT Madras
Connecting Mumbai to Pune instead of JNPT to Vadhavan Port

claimText: Hyperloop technology relies on a magnetic levitation (maglev) system operating inside low-pressure sealed tubes.
evidenceExcerpt: Hyperloop uses a magnetic levitation (maglev) system inside low-pressure sealed tubes.
examUse: Science and Technology statement-based questions testing the fundamental working principles of Hyperloop.
distractorHooks: Aerodynamic lift instead of magnetic levitation
Open-air tracks instead of sealed tubes

claimText: Hyperloop systems utilize Linear Induction Motors (LIM) for silent propulsion, allowing pods to reach theoretical speeds of up to 1,200 km/h.
evidenceExcerpt: It utilizes Linear Induction Motors (LIM) for silent propulsion, enabling theoretical speeds of 1,200 km/h.
examUse: Science and Technology questions focusing on the propulsion mechanisms and speed capabilities of advanced mobility systems.
distractorHooks: Rotary electric motors instead of Linear Induction Motors

claimText: The key components of a hyperloop system include steel tubes, pressurized capsules, compressors, and air bearing suspension.
evidenceExcerpt: Key components include steel tubes, pressurized capsules, compressors, and air bearing suspension.
examUse: Multiple-choice questions asking to identify the correct components of a Hyperloop system.
distractorHooks: Rubber tire suspension instead of air bearing suspension
Concrete open channels instead of steel tubes
